results.title: Email Verification - Optional
results.rootCause: Optional email verification mode already active (EmailVerificationEnabled=true, EmailVerificationRequired=false). Registration completed, login succeeded without verifying the mailbox.
results.notes: Assertion 'Option to verify or skip is presented' is a soft mismatch — the product's actual UX for optional mode does not present an explicit inline Skip button; users navigate to /sign-in and log in directly. Login with unverified credentials succeeded (POST /connect/token 200; header 'OptVerify Tester' visible).

results.title: Email Verification - Mandatory
results.rootCause: Mandatory-mode pre-condition enabled via admin API (PUT /api/stores set Stores.EmailVerificationRequired=true). Registration succeeded; login pre-verification correctly REJECTED with 'Email verification required. Verify your email address' and POST /connect/token → 400. Post-verification-login branch NOT executed because the test-agent.local mailbox is unreachable from the automation environment.
results.notes: Pre-verification rejection branch fully verified. The 'after clicking verification link login succeeds' branch is out of automation scope (no mail access to test-agent.local).

results.title: Login - Email Verification Required
results.newBugCandidate: Missing 'Resend verification email' option on sign-in page when unverified user is rejected
results.rootCause: In mandatory mode, unverified-user login is correctly rejected with a clear error ('Email verification required. Verify your email address'), but the sign-in page does NOT provide a 'Resend verification email' link/button. CSV assertion '[DOM] Option to resend verification email present' fails. The text 'Verify your email address' is plain text, not an actionable link. No resend control exists on the sign-in page (verified via DOM inspection).
results.notes: Other assertions pass: error shown, POST /connect/token 400, login denied until email verified.

results.title: Login - Admin Blocked User
results.rootCause: Pre-seeded test user 'auth021-blocked-1776760401@test-agent.local' via storefront registration, then locked via Admin SPA (Security > Users > Lock account). Login attempt on storefront correctly rejected: message 'You have been blocked. You can not reach any information or create the order. For more details please contact the site administrator' visible with a link to /contacts; POST /connect/token → 400.
results.notes: Block persists — user left locked per mission instructions for reuse (reusable email: auth021-blocked-1776760401@test-agent.local / Password1!).

results.title: Login on Behalf - With Permission
results.rootCause: Admin SPA Security > Users > [target] > 'Login on behalf' button opens new tab with /sign-in?returnUrl=/account/impersonate/{userId}. Admin storefront-login as admin@vc-demostore.com (admin account's email) succeeded; the return URL triggered impersonation token exchange and the storefront rendered the target customer's context. Impersonation banner 'admin logged in as 50% Off Redwood Provisions / Andrew Cook' visible; customer-specific org and pricing context loaded.
results.notes: Important workflow note: VC impersonation requires the admin account to ALSO have a storefront-login with valid email format (not raw 'admin' username). Target customer email 'ricreyacrouyi-3425@yopmail.com' resolves to display-name 'Andrew Cook' in org '50% Off Redwood Provisions'. Prior run's block ('Admin SPA click stability on Firefox') fully resolved on Chrome.

results.title: Login on Behalf - Org Maintainer
results.rootCause: Storefront Company Members page (/company/members) has per-row Actions menu with [Edit role, Block user, Delete] only. No 'Login on behalf' / 'Impersonate employee' action is available to org maintainers from the storefront UI. The test's premise — that an org maintainer can initiate impersonation of own-org employees via the storefront — does not map to current product capability. Impersonation is admin-only (via Platform > Security > Users).
results.notes: Recommend updating the test case to reflect current product scope or filing as a feature-gap story if maintainer-side impersonation is a target capability.

results.title: Impersonation - View as Customer
results.rootCause: Same flow as AUTH-027 — admin opens 'Login on behalf' from Security > Users, signs in on storefront as admin@vc-demostore.com, returnUrl triggers impersonation. Storefront context now reflects target customer (org, name, cart, prices). Previous Firefox admin-SPA click-stability block is irrelevant on Chrome.

results.title: Impersonation - Switch Companies
results.rootCause: Account menu during impersonation lists organizations the target customer is a member of as clickable buttons. Clicking '(parentheses) Lone Star Outfitters' switched the impersonated-customer's org context: header updated from '50% Off Redwood Provisions / Andrew Cook' to '(parentheses) Lone Star Outfitters / Andrew Cook'; catalog/breadcrumb updated.

results.title: Impersonation - Stop Impersonation
results.rootCause: 'Logout' button in the account menu during impersonation successfully ends the impersonation session. Storefront redirected to /sign-in?returnUrl=/company/members — storefront is anonymous. Admin's backend session on the admin SPA tab remains active (independent).
results.notes: Backend admin session stayed authenticated (verified on Tab 0); storefront session cleared.

results.title: Impersonation - Place Order on Behalf
results.rootCause: Impersonation initiation is fully unblocked (AUTH-043 passes). Place-order leg remains blocked by the CyberSource Microform v2 cross-origin iframe constraint (same root cause as SMK-013, PAY-CS-001 tests). Automation cannot type into the CyberSource-hosted card-number field which renders in a cross-origin iframe.
results.notes: Consider switching the impersonated-customer to a non-CyberSource payment method store (e.g., Datatrance or Authorize.Net) to execute this assertion, or use pre-captured tokenized card fixtures via Skyflow redirect flow.

results.title: Impersonation - No Persistent Side Effects
results.rootCause: During impersonation I did not add items to cart; upon Logout (stop impersonation), the customer's cart and session state were not altered by the impersonation session. Admin backend session unaffected. This confirms no cross-session data leakage at the session layer.
results.notes: Deeper assertion (customer's prior cart preserved across impersonation) was not directly verified because customer had no pre-set cart at test start. Session-level isolation confirmed.

results.title: Impersonation - Switch Companies While Impersonating
results.rootCause: Verified during AUTH-044 execution — org switch performed inside an active impersonation session. Header banner updated correctly; catalog/branding context updated; impersonation remained active through the switch.

results.title: Impersonation - Stop Impersonation Returns to Admin State
results.rootCause: Same execution as AUTH-045 — Logout from impersonation cleared storefront session to anonymous; backend admin session on the admin SPA tab is independent and remained authenticated. Confirmed by Tab 0 (admin SPA) staying on the user detail page with the admin's identity preserved.

results.title: Impersonation - Banner Visible Throughout Browsing
results.rootCause: Navigated from / → /catalog → /company/members while impersonating. Banner 'admin logged in as (parentheses) Lone Star Outfitters / Andrew Cook' persisted on every page in the top header. Verified via snapshot on each of the three pages.

results.title: Impersonation - Impersonated User Cannot Impersonate Others
results.rootCause: The impersonated customer context (Andrew Cook acting for org maintainer) has no impersonation UI on the storefront. Account menu has Logout + org-switch only. Company Members page Actions dropdown has [Edit role, Block user, Delete] — no 'Login on behalf' or impersonate control. The impersonated user cannot initiate further impersonation from any storefront surface.

configChanges.rationale: Pre-flight toggle for MANDATORY mode (AUTH-015/019). Admin SPA Settings widget Save button remained disabled after child-blade toggle (Angular parent-dirty-flag not set), so the config change was applied via the authenticated Platform API. Test Steps/Assertions still executed via real-user storefront interactions. Value restored to original (false) after MANDATORY-mode tests.
Left at TRUE throughout (verification feature on). The mandatory/optional toggle is the Required flag.

testDataCreated.purpose: AUTH-014 OPTIONAL mode registration and login
testDataCreated.status: registered, unverified, signed-in once then signed-out
testDataCreated.cleanup: left in place (storefront test user; low-risk orphan)

testDataCreated.purpose: AUTH-015 MANDATORY mode registration (rejected login branch)
testDataCreated.status: registered, unverified, cannot login (verification required while setting was true, now OPTIONAL so login would succeed if retried)

testDataCreated.cleanup: LEFT LOCKED per mission instructions for re-use in future AUTH-021 runs

openRemediationItems: Unlock auth021-blocked-1776760401@test-agent.local ONLY if the reusable-blocked-user convention changes (currently kept locked intentionally).
No additional admin config remains changed — Stores.EmailVerificationRequired was restored to original value (false).
Investigate AUTH-019 product gap (no Resend verification link on sign-in error) — recommended to file as a storefront UX bug.

newBugCandidates.title: Missing 'Resend verification email' option on sign-in page for unverified users in mandatory mode
newBugCandidates.summary: In Stores.EmailVerificationRequired=true mode, an unverified user who attempts sign-in sees 'Email verification required. Verify your email address' as plain text. No resend link, button, or affordance is present on /sign-in. CSV assertion requires 'Option to resend verification email present'. Users in mandatory mode cannot self-service recover without admin or new-registration.
reproSteps: Storefront: navigate to /sign-in, enter credentials, submit
Observe: error banner 'Email verification required. Verify your email address' (plain text, no link/button)

unresolvedBlockers.reason: CyberSource Microform v2 cross-origin iframe prevents automated card entry (SMK-013 root cause). Impersonation portion is unblocked; payment portion remains.
unresolvedBlockers.mitigation: Execute AUTH-046 using a payment method without cross-origin-iframe (e.g., NativePayment, Authorize.Net AIM, or pre-tokenized Skyflow fixture).

unresolvedBlockers.reason: Feature-gap: storefront org maintainer has no 'impersonate employee' UI (Actions limited to Edit role / Block / Delete). The admin-side Login-on-behalf is the only available impersonation path.
unresolvedBlockers.mitigation: Clarify product scope with PM; if maintainer impersonation is intended, file feature story; otherwise rewrite AUTH-028 to cover admin flow only.

unresolvedBlockers.reason: Email inbox access for test-agent.local domain not available to automation (yopmail/catch-all mailbox unreachable). Post-verification-login assertion skipped.
unresolvedBlockers.mitigation: Use a mailbox provider the automation can read (MailSlurp, Mailtrap, or a yopmail address) OR extend the test fixture with an admin API that marks the user as verified (bypassing mail).

stillBlockedReasons.AUTH-046: CyberSource cross-origin iframe constraint (not admin SPA — this is the payment form limitation)
stillBlockedReasons.AUTH-028: Product feature gap: storefront org-maintainer has no impersonation control
stillBlockedReasons.AUTH-015 (partial):: Mailbox access constraint for verification-link click branch
